steps.title: Compare the proportions
steps.content: The sample proportions are $54\%$ and $46\%$. These are equally far from $50\%$, so they do not explain the large difference in margin of error.

steps.title: Use sample size effect
steps.content: With the same population and method, a larger sample size gives a smaller margin of error. A smaller sample size gives a larger margin of error.

steps.title: Match the larger margin
steps.content:
Sample A has the larger margin of error, $3.8\%$, compared with Sample B's $1.4\%$. Therefore Sample A must have had the smaller sample size.
The answer is D.
